text: at home while growth has been reasonably satisfactory unemployment and inflation continue at excessive levels
llm_diagnosis: Abel's Markov blanket locks onto 'inflation at excessive levels' and routes to a hawkish tightening response, but the speaker is describing persistent stagflation where unemployment is ALSO excessive - the dovish read captures concern about jobs, not inflation-fighting resolve.

text: mortgages with some of the characteristics of nontraditional mortgage products have been available for many years however they have historically been offered to higherincome borrowers
llm_diagnosis: Abel misses the implicit warning about nontraditional mortgage products expanding down-market to riskier borrowers (a financial stability concern that maps hawkish); the benign phrasing 'available for many years' misleads Abel toward a dovish accommodation signal.

text: second growth could remain above trend and the unemployment rate could continue to fall while at the same time inflation fell still further
llm_diagnosis: Abel sees 'inflation fell' and 'unemployment fall' as dovish disinflation with room to ease, but this is actually a description of overheating-without-inflation (above-trend growth) which implies the economy is running hot - a hawkish watch-for-risks stance.

text: in addition stimulus payments and a year of reduced spending might have given workers the financial wherewithal to be more selective about pay working conditions or health risks
llm_diagnosis: Abel reads 'stimulus payments' and 'reduced spending' through the demand-slack lens (dovish), missing that worker selectivity over pay creates wage pressure and labor supply tightness - an institutional wage-push channel that is hawkish.

text: a rising proportion of the population is also taking advantage of both general adult education and workrelated instruction
llm_diagnosis: Abel over-flattens human capital/education content into the social-spending dovish bucket; the Markov blanket has no node for productivity/supply-side growth, so rising skill investment (hawkish via potential-output expansion) gets inverted.

text: we need to pay special attention to the workers affected by such change because this dynamic process helps generate ever higher standards of living
llm_diagnosis: Abel latches onto 'workers affected' and 'special attention' as labor-market protection (dovish), missing that the speaker endorses creative-destruction dynamics producing higher living standards - a pro-growth hawkish-leaning supply-side message.

text: equality of economic opportunity appeals to our sense of fairness certainly but it also strengthens our economy
llm_diagnosis: Abel's keyword heuristic maps 'fairness/equality' to dovish social concern, but the economic argument is about strengthening productive capacity - Abel lacks a supply-side labor-participation node so it inverts the intent.

text: given the lags in the effects of monetary policy that means we will not be able to wait until the unemployment rate is down close to its longterm level
llm_diagnosis: Abel reads 'cannot wait' plus 'unemployment' as pre-emptive tightening (hawkish), but in context the speaker is explaining why they will NOT wait for full employment before easing - a dovish preemption-of-softness message Abel's Markov blanket misreads.

text: it has been an advantage to the bank and has enabled the bank better to serve its conmrunity
llm_diagnosis: Abel has no causal node for 'bank functioning well serving community' (a pro-credit-system institutional positive) and defaults the positive-for-banks framing to dovish ease rather than hawkish confidence in financial system strength.

text: in addition households that have worked down debt levels will be less vulnerable and better able to withstand shocks in the future
llm_diagnosis: Abel interprets 'worked down debt' as household deleveraging implying weak demand (dovish), missing that reduced household vulnerability increases economic resilience and removes a financial-stability constraint on tightening (hawkish).

text: this type of performance positions the community banking sector for continued success this year and well into the future helping ensure the preservation of the community bank model for future generati
llm_diagnosis: Abel lacks an institutional concept for 'community bank health = credit supply strength = hawkish confidence'; the 'preservation' and 'future generations' language triggers a protective/dovish stance instead of a strength endorsement.

text: jumptarts mission to develop a more financially literate population supports the federal reserves goal of a stable and growing economy
llm_diagnosis: Abel has no node for financial-literacy-as-stability-enabler; 'supports' + 'population' reads as accommodative social policy, whereas the actual frame endorses the Fed's stable-growth mandate (a hawkish institutional affirmation).

text: there is an overwhelming amount of research that demonstrates the importance of educational attainment in helping cities grow and thrive
llm_diagnosis: Education/human-capital growth arguments map to supply-side potential (hawkish via higher sustainable output), but Abel's Markov blanket routes 'educational attainment' through social-spending/dovish keywords.

text: as people get laid off from jobs with big companies they often start their own new businesses helping to keep the economy resilient
llm_diagnosis: Abel fixates on 'laid off' as labor-market weakness (dovish), missing that the sentence frames job losses as catalysts for entrepreneurship and RESILIENCE - a pro-flexibility, hawkish-tilted narrative about productive churn.
